text.title: Agent Drift Detection (Statistical) Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example applying agent-drift-detection-statistical to a representative case. Walks step-by-step through the procedure, shows the produced artefact, and highlights the rules it satisfies.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Context: a team that matches the Applies-If conditions for agent-drift-detection-statistical. Inputs are populated per the Prerequisites table.

list.item: Step 1: Compute baseline pass-rate + output distribution from last green eval run. — produced baseline stats.
Step 2: Configure CUSUM detector with k=0.5, h=4. — produced CUSUM live.
Step 3: Configure KL drift detector with yellow/red thresholds. — produced KL live.
Step 4: Add paired t-test on judge score per metric; apply Bonferroni. — produced regression test.
Step 5: Wire alert routing per severity band; retest policy per r5. — produced alerts live.
Step 6: Document runbook: 'when this alert fires, do that'. — produced runbook.

section.title: Final artefact
section.p: The team commits the final artefact (per spec shape) at the version-controlled path declared in the output contract. Owner signs and links it to downstream consumers.

list.item: r1: Pass-rate control chart uses CUSUM with k=0.5 and h=4 (standard EWMA defaults); alert when CUSUM exceeds h.
r2: Output-distribution drift is measured by KL divergence over a categorical bucketing of the output (intent, schema-key se
r3: Judge-score regression uses a paired t-test against baseline; significance threshold p<0.05 with Bonferroni correctio
r4: Drift alert severity: yellow (KL ≥ 0.05 OR CUSUM ≥ 2), red (KL ≥ 0.1 OR CUSUM ≥ 4 OR p<0.01 regression).
r5: Every alert triggers a retest with a 2x sample size; if retest is also red, freeze deploys; if yellow, course-correct wi
